Article.ArticleTitle: Anti-CSF-1R therapy with combined immuno-chemotherapy coordinate an adaptive immune response to eliminate macrophage enriched triple negative breast cancers.

Abstract.AbstractText: Women diagnosed with metastatic triple negative breast cancer (mTNBC) have limited treatment options, are more prone to develop resistance and are associated with high mortality. A cold tumor immune microenvironment (TIME) characterized by low T cells and high tumor associated macrophages (TAMs) in mTNBC is associated with the failure of standard-of-care chemotherapy and immune checkpoint blockade (ICB) treatment. We demonstrate that the combination of immunomodulatory low-dose Cyclophosphamide (CTX) coupled with anti-CSF-1R antibody targeted therapy (SNDX-ms6352) and anti-PD-1 (ICB), was highly effective against aggressive metastatic Trp53 null TNBC transplantable syngeneic models that present with high macrophage infiltration. Mechanistically, CSF-1R inhibition along with CTX disrupted the M-CSF/CSF-1R axis which upregulated IL-17, IL-5 and type II interferon resulting in elevated B- and T cell infiltration. Addition of an anti-PD-1 maintenance dose helped overcome de novo PD-L1 intra-tumoral heterogeneity (ITH) associated recurrence in lung and liver mTNBC.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Competing interests: C.M.P. is an equity stockholder of and consultant for BioClassifier LLC. C.M.P. is also listed as an inventor on a patent application (US9631239B2) for the Breast PAM50 Subtyping assay (not related to this work). The remaining authors declare no competing interests.
